smell_name: Gradients Not Cleared Before Backward Propagation
description: optimizer.zero_grad() is absent before loss.backward(), causing gradients to accumulate across iterations from all backward() calls, leading to exploding gradients and failed training.
fix_strategy: Insert optimizer.zero_grad() at the correct position before loss.backward() within the training loop. The correct position must be inferred from control flow — it should be inside the training loop, before the forward pass or immediately before loss.backward().

canonical_examples.before:
for epoch in range(num_epochs):
    out = model(X)
    loss = criterion(out, y)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
canonical_examples.after:
for epoch in range(num_epochs):
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    out = model(X)
    loss = criterion(out, y)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()

false_positive_patterns.description: zero_grad() is correctly placed at the top of the loop body but separated from loss.backward() by intervening statements such as the forward pass and loss computation.
false_positive_patterns.example:
for epoch in range(epochs):
    optimizer.zero_grad()  # correctly placed
    out = net(torch_X)
    loss = criterion(out, torch_y)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()

false_positive_patterns.description: Gradient accumulation is architecturally intentional, controlled by a runtime condition or a nested minibatch loop within a custom backward hook.

verification_check: Verify that optimizer.zero_grad() now appears at the correct position relative to loss.backward() in the training loop — it must be inside the loop and before the backward call.
